research_question: Does a TIME-CONDITIONAL early exit (cut stagnant/non-favored 0DTE positions at 15:00/15:15/15:30, let in-favor positions ride to existing exits) beat the 15:50 hard time stop on real-fills, WITHOUT hurting J's anchor-day winners? Game Plan 2 — exit refinement around the theta cliff.

exit_grid.fixed: premium_stop_pct=-0.99 (chart-stop only); tp1_premium 0.30; tp1_qty_fraction 0.50; runner 2.5; level_stop_buffer 0.50

gate_definition: PROMOTE-CANDIDATE iff vs same-population same-strike 15:50 baseline: total P&L AND expectancy strictly improve AND anchor edge_capture >= baseline (anchor-no-regression) AND DSR/PSR advisory != FAIL AND anchor not vacuous. Honest no-win is a valid result.

ATM.verdict: [ATM] BASELINE-OPTIMAL — NO time-conditional cutoff beat the 15:50 baseline on both total P&L and expectancy. Cutting non-favored positions earlier does NOT help this population/strike (baseline total $-5957.8, exp $-44.13). 15:50 is already right here — earlier exits clip late winners / give up theta-rebound. Honest no-win (a valuable result).

ITM2.verdict: [ITM2] BASELINE-OPTIMAL — NO time-conditional cutoff beat the 15:50 baseline on both total P&L and expectancy. Cutting non-favored positions earlier does NOT help this population/strike (baseline total $-14240.3, exp $-115.77). 15:50 is already right here — earlier exits clip late winners / give up theta-rebound. Honest no-win (a valuable result).

ATM.verdict: [ATM] BETTER-BUT-GATE-FAILS — best time-conditional config (cutoff 15:00 / favor +0%) beats baseline on total $-2749.8 vs $-3265.8 but fails the DSR FAIL leg. No clean PROMOTE-CANDIDATE.

ITM2.verdict: [ITM2] BETTER-BUT-GATE-FAILS — best time-conditional config (cutoff 15:00 / favor +25%) beats baseline on total $-11455.3 vs $-12830.2 but fails the anchor regression leg. No clean PROMOTE-CANDIDATE.

op20_disclosures.authority: Real-fills (lib.simulator_real + OPRA options cache, valid through 2026-05-29) is the only authority used for the verdict (C1). BS-sim / SPY-space grade not used.
op20_disclosures.anchor_coverage: THIN. 5/04 (J's biggest winner, +$730) has ZERO option bars in the cache, so it cannot be graded on real fills; BRM has an OPRA fill on only 4/29 among the WIN anchor days. The anchor-no-regression gate is necessary-not-sufficient (C24) — a tie at n=1 anchor fill is weak evidence.
op20_disclosures.level_source: Historically-rebuilt ★★ proxies (active+multi_day from _detect_from_history as-of each day), NOT production ★★★ named levels (no archive). Absolute WR is a proxy LOWER-BOUND; the EXIT comparison is internally consistent (identical signal population + level set across all variants), so the sign-flip question is answerable.
op20_disclosures.favor_test: 'In favor' at the cutoff = TP1 filled OR current-bar premium >= entry*(1+thr). Uses the current bar HIGH as the favorable reference (generous — only genuinely stagnant positions are cut). Evaluated strictly BEFORE the 15:50 stop.
op20_disclosures.knob: Opt-in lib.simulator_real.simulate_trade_real(early_cutoff_et, early_cutoff_min_favor_pct); defaults early_cutoff_et=None => OFF => byte-for-byte prior behavior (verified: 18 e2e tests + anchor reproduction unchanged). RESEARCH ONLY — no params/doctrine changed (Rule 9).
op20_disclosures.populations: PRIMARY = BEARISH_REJECTION_MORNING (the codified CONFIRMED setup, the prior bearish-continuation scorecard's clean edge). BROAD = pooled BRM + LEVEL_BREAK_FIRST_STRIKE + HEAD_AND_SHOULDERS_BEAR real-fills (same set that scorecard graded), to see the exit effect across the whole bearish-continuation book.
op20_disclosures.dsr_returns: DSR/PSR on per-trade dollar P&L (constant qty=3 notional -> comparable). PBO skipped (no CSCV matrix). n_trials=9 (grid size). Advisory only (gate.py is not a hard gate).

Cutting non-favored positions earlier does NOT help this population/strike (baseline total $-5957.8, exp $-44.13). 15:50 is already right here — earlier exits clip late winners / give up theta-rebound. overall_verdict.summary: 15:50 BASELINE IS OPTIMAL across all tested populations/strikes — no earlier time-conditional cutoff beat it on both total P&L and expectancy with anchor-no-regression. Cutting non-favored positions earlier clips late winners / gives up theta-rebound. Honest no-win (a valuable result): do NOT move the time stop earlier on this evidence.
